notes: Generated by skills/playbook-course/scripts/generate-curriculum.mjs. Do not edit by hand. Re-run after manifest.yml changes.

modules.title: The File System
modules.outcome: Navigate folders, read paths, recognize extensions, and know where you are before running a command.

modules.title: Terminal fluency
modules.outcome: Use the small set of Terminal commands and shortcuts needed for setup, git, and Claude Code.

modules.title: Git as save-points
modules.outcome: Use branches, commits, push, pull, status, and diff as the save-point system behind pull requests.

modules.title: Your auth setup
modules.outcome: Name the auth layers behind Claude Code, recognise which one is failing, and know which fixes are yours vs. the program's.

modules.title: Installing the stack
modules.outcome: Install and verify the White Belt tool stack without drifting away from the program-pinned setup path.

modules.title: The LLM Gateway
modules.outcome: Understand what the LiteLLM gateway does, what Claude Code does, and which failures are yours to debug vs. the gateway's.

modules.title: Compass plugin
modules.outcome: Understand what the program-pinned plugin is for, how to verify it, and how to avoid stale-plugin drift.

modules.title: GREEN / YELLOW / RED
modules.outcome: Run the 10-point health check, interpret colour honestly, and route setup failures without wasting a cohort day.

modules.title: Your first conversation with Claude Code
modules.outcome: Start Claude Code safely, ask a scoped first question, and review the answer before allowing edits.

modules.title: Prompt quality 101
modules.outcome: Turn vague asks into scoped prompts with goal, context, constraints, and success criteria.

modules.title: The permission system
modules.outcome: Respond safely to yes, no, and always-style permission prompts without handing control away.

modules.title: Your first PR
modules.outcome: Move one reviewed local change from branch to pull request with a clear title, description, and reviewer path.

quests.title: Quest W-0: Turn GREEN
quests.outcome: Run setup verification, reach all-GREEN status, and capture evidence that the local environment is ready.

quests.title: Quest W-1: The HelloRazorpay commit
quests.outcome: Open and close a tiny sandbox PR that proves branch, commit, push, and PR mechanics.

boss_fight.title: Boss Fight W-B: One real typo, merged
boss_fight.outcome: Find one genuine typo or equivalent micro-fix, use AI assistance to triage it, and land the merged PR.

belts.title: Yellow Belt
belts.promise: I build with AI daily. I find and fix things in the surfaces I care about.

modules.title: Tool Atlas in practice
modules.outcome: Pick the right AI surface for the job you're about to do, by working through three real decision examples.

modules.title: When to reach for which tool
modules.outcome: Use a simple decision tree to choose the right AI surface for a task before starting.

modules.title: Prompt quality, deep dive
modules.outcome: Write prompts that express intent, constraints, context, and success criteria strongly enough for daily repo work.

modules.title: What Claude can see and what it cannot
modules.outcome: Understand local files, pasted context, connector context, and the limits of what Claude can see.

modules.title: CLAUDE.md primer
modules.outcome: Understand CLAUDE.md as a project rule book and know what belongs in it.

modules.title: Reading unfamiliar code with Claude
modules.outcome: Use Claude Code to orient in an unfamiliar repo without immediately editing or over-reading.

modules.title: Permissions, hooks, slash commands
modules.outcome: Use permissions, hooks, and slash commands as daily safety rails instead of speed hacks.

modules.title: LiteLLM and Claude workspace access
modules.outcome: Choose the Claude workspace or LiteLLM route you have actually been provisioned for.

modules.title: Figma MCP for non-engineers
modules.outcome: Use design connector context to move from a Figma frame to a small, reviewable code change.

modules.title: Slack MCP + Google Workspace MCP
modules.outcome: Use messaging and workspace connectors to gather task context safely and summarize it for repo work.

modules.title: Bug hunting with AI
modules.outcome: Use AI, git history, connectors, and repo reading to find a small real bug worth fixing.

modules.title: Debugging with Claude
modules.outcome: Use Claude to run a disciplined reproduce, observe, hypothesize, isolate, fix, verify debugging loop.

modules.title: PR craft
modules.outcome: Write small, reviewable PRs with clear titles, descriptions, staged commits, and reviewer etiquette.

modules.title: Staying current
modules.outcome: Set up a light change-awareness habit without drowning in changelogs or tool updates.

quests.title: Quest Y-1: Ship the Stand-up Bot
quests.outcome: Build a small working utility with Claude Code, demo it, and explain the prompt, context, and harness decisions.

quests.title: Quest Y-2: The 30-day 2-minute challenge
quests.outcome: Build a lightweight daily AI practice log that proves habit, reflection, and learning without becoming busywork.

boss_fight.title: Yellow Belt boss fight
boss_fight.outcome: Find an open bug in your area, fix it with AI assistance, and open the PR that earns Yellow Belt.

modules.title: The Three Pillars — prompt × context × harness
modules.outcome: Diagnose any AI coding session by naming which pillar is weak — prompt, context, or harness — and improve that pillar specifically.

modules.title: Why context windows fill — the single constraint everything else follows
modules.outcome: Internalise the context-window constraint and use it to make better decisions about what to load, what to summarise, and what to leave out.

modules.title: CLAUDE.md for a real service — WHAT + WHY, under 200 lines
modules.outcome: Write a CLAUDE.md that names what the service is, what matters, and what to avoid — under 200 lines, with WHY beside every rule.

modules.title: Hierarchical CLAUDE.md in a monorepo — root vs package vs local
modules.outcome: Place the right rule at the right level — root, package, or local — so each session reads only the rules that apply to its working directory.

modules.title: CLAUDE.local.md — personal overrides, and what belongs there
modules.outcome: Use CLAUDE.local.md for personal overrides without polluting the team's shared CLAUDE.md.

modules.title: Skills — what they are, why they compound
modules.outcome: Recognise when a workflow has earned a place in the skills library and understand why skills are the second compound interest in AI productivity after CLAUDE.md.

modules.title: Writing your first SKILL.md — the anatomy, naming, discipline
modules.outcome: Write a SKILL.md that triggers reliably, does a bounded job, and earns a place in the team's skill library.

modules.title: Subagents — when to delegate, how to pass intent cleanly
modules.outcome: Decide when a task is better delegated to a subagent than handled in the main session, and pass intent to that subagent without losing context.

modules.title: Worktrees — running 3-5 Claude instances in parallel, safely
modules.outcome: Run multiple Claude Code sessions against the same repo at the same time without conflicting edits, broken builds, or confused state.

modules.title: Hooks + slash commands — when to automate the pre-flight
modules.outcome: Use hooks to enforce pre-flight checks automatically, and slash commands to give yourself reliable shortcuts; know which is the right shape for which job.

modules.title: Advanced prompting — goals, constraints, worked examples
modules.outcome: Write prompts that name the goal, the constraints, and the success criteria precisely enough that the agent's output is consistent across runs and worth shipping.

modules.title: E2E testing with Playwright + Claude Code
modules.outcome: Write a Playwright end-to-end test with Claude Code that survives the codebase, reads cleanly, and catches the regression you actually fear.

modules.title: The Playwright Skill pattern — one-shot test quality
modules.outcome: Package the Playwright spec-then-code loop into a skill that turns a behaviour description into a working test in one invocation.

modules.title: tests/seed.spec.ts — saving the agent 10,000 tokens
modules.outcome: Use a seed spec — a single small Playwright test that exercises the canonical fixtures, helpers, and conventions — to anchor the agent's understanding of the test directory in one read.

modules.title: Design-to-code — Figma + Blade + Code Connect, end to end
modules.outcome: Walk a Figma frame through the Figma connector, the Blade design system, and Code Connect into running code that respects design-system conventions and ships cleanly.

modules.title: Blade deep dive — tokens, primitives, variants, accessibility
modules.outcome: Understand Blade's anatomy — tokens, primitives, variants, accessibility — well enough to pick the right component for any UI need without inventing one.

modules.title: The production-compiler skill — AI-Studio / ChatGPT output → Blade
modules.outcome: Recognise when AI-generated code needs to be repaired against the design system, and use the production-compiler skill to bring the code back to Blade primitives.

modules.title: Node + pnpm + localhost + mobile viewport — the daily loop
modules.outcome: Run a tight, fast local-dev loop for frontend work — install, dev server, viewport switching — that scales across multiple worktrees and surfaces issues before commit.

modules.title: Branch-preview platform — branch → live URL
modules.outcome: Use branch-preview URLs to share work in progress with design and product reviewers without anyone having to check out your branch.

modules.title: Observability with AI — logs, traces, cost attribution
modules.outcome: Use Claude Code with the observability connectors to triage production-shape issues — logs, traces, and cost attribution — without dumping raw output into the chat window.

modules.title: Debugging the hard kind — when Claude is wrong, and you have to tell
modules.outcome: Recognise when Claude's analysis is wrong, push back productively, and get to the right answer without falling into either capitulation or argument.

modules.title: What never goes into a prompt — the Razorpay redlines
modules.outcome: Internalise the things that must never appear in a prompt — the redlines — and the reflexes that catch them before you hit Enter.

modules.title: The LLM proxy — what LiteLLM does and why every call routes through it
modules.outcome: Understand why every model call routes through a proxy, what LiteLLM gives you that direct calls do not, and how to debug when the proxy is the friction.

modules.title: PII, PCI, RBI — the regulators, the reasons, the reflexes
modules.outcome: Recognise data that falls under PII, PCI, or RBI scope; understand why each regulator cares; develop the reflexes that keep regulator-protected data out of AI-assisted work.

modules.title: Prompt injection + output classifiers — the threats, the mitigations
modules.outcome: Recognise prompt-injection patterns, understand what output classifiers catch and what they miss, and design AI-assisted features so injection cannot escalate.

modules.title: The pre-ship-check skill — 6-layer gate before every PR
modules.outcome: Run the pre-ship-check skill before every PR, read the six-layer report, and fix what it surfaces — without working around the gate.

modules.title: The Blade-compliance reviewer skill — file-granularity checks
modules.outcome: Run the Blade-compliance reviewer skill on individual UI files to catch design-system drift the pre-ship-check's PR-level scan might miss.

modules.title: Using a subagent for security review
modules.outcome: Spawn a subagent for security review with a clean brief, consume the structured findings, and act on what is real without wasting context on noise.

quests.title: Quest G-1: Author a team skill
quests.outcome: Capture a real team workflow as a SKILL.md, get one teammate to invoke it on a real task, and log the invocation.

quests.title: Quest G-2: The Greenfield cross-over
quests.outcome: Ship one meaningful change in a greenfield surface that exercises Part A craft and Part B practices together — and capture the cross-over in the reflection.

boss_fight.title: Boss Fight G-B: The double-ship
boss_fight.outcome: Ship two merged PRs — one on a product repo with five named sub-requirements, plus your Quest G-2 greenfield PR — and earn Green Belt.

modules.title: Authoring an internal MCP server — architecture, auth, packaging
modules.outcome: Author an internal MCP server with sound architecture, real auth, and a packaging story other PODs can install.

modules.title: Publishing a skill pack — naming, versioning, governance
modules.outcome: Bundle one or more SKILL.md artefacts into a pack and publish it so other PODs can install, pin, and adopt it.

modules.title: Building a plugin marketplace entry for Razorpay Cowork
modules.outcome: Publish a plugin to the program's Cowork tenant marketplace so non-engineer teammates can install your skill pack with one click.

modules.title: The Claude Agent SDK — when to write your own agent
modules.outcome: Decide cleanly between extending the program-pinned plugin and writing a custom agent with the Claude Agent SDK.

modules.title: Multi-agent orchestration — patterns that work, patterns that don't
modules.outcome: Choose the right multi-agent orchestration pattern for the job and refuse the patterns that consistently fail.

modules.title: Tool design — JSON schemas, output shapes, error contracts
modules.outcome: Design tool contracts — input schemas, output shapes, error shapes — that compose, scale, and survive version changes.

modules.title: Progressive disclosure — skills that stay small
modules.outcome: Design skills that stay small by progressively disclosing their depth — short by default, deeper when invoked, deepest when needed.

modules.title: Memory systems — auto-memory, session state, long-running agents
modules.outcome: Choose the right memory shape for an agent's job — auto-memory, session state, or external storage.

modules.title: Prompt evals — A/B, regression, golden sets
modules.outcome: Run prompt evaluations against your shipped skills with the right shape — golden sets for regression, A/B for improvements, named pass criteria.

modules.title: Cost attribution + observability at team + org scale
modules.outcome: Instrument cost and observability at team and org scale, not just per-session — and identify the patterns that run quietly expensive.

modules.title: Effort settings, model routing, fall-backs
modules.outcome: Tune per-call settings — effort level, model choice, fall-back path — to match the work's stakes.

modules.title: Running office hours — the Whoop / Ramp pattern
modules.outcome: Run a weekly office hour that surfaces blockers across a POD set and produces a redacted decision log.

modules.title: Embedded sprints — the CTO-with-a-team week
modules.outcome: Embed for a week with a POD outside your own and ship with the team rather than for it; produce a written debrief.

modules.title: Writing an AI RFC — what good looks like at Razorpay
modules.outcome: Write an AI RFC that earns a merge by naming what would change and how to measure whether it worked.

modules.title: Contributing to the API Council (AI-specific reviews)
modules.outcome: Contribute to API Council reviews on AI-specific submissions and apply API design discipline to MCP and agent surfaces.

modules.title: Plugin + skill governance — approval, deprecation, security review
modules.outcome: Govern plugin and skill lifecycles cleanly across approval, deprecation, and security review.

quests.title: Quest B-1: Publish an internal plugin
quests.outcome: Author and publish a skill pack so at least two PODs outside your team install it within a month.

quests.title: Quest B-2: Component contribution or full-stack feature
quests.outcome: Prove cross-layer ownership by either contributing a Blade component via the contribution pipeline or shipping a full-stack feature you own end-to-end.

boss_fight.title: Boss Fight B-B: Own a POD's AI uplift for a month
boss_fight.outcome: Embed with a POD outside your own for a month, intervene with the full Black Belt toolkit, ship a signed-off metric lift and a one-pager case study.

belts.title: Staff+ Council
belts.promise: I shape the program over multi-year horizons through Council membership, RFC sponsorship, mentoring, and external representation.

modules.title: What this is and is not
modules.outcome: Understand what the Council is and is not, why senior-IC communities exist, and what membership represents.

modules.title: Structure: charter, cadence, membership
modules.outcome: Understand how the Council is composed, how it meets, how it interfaces with engineering leadership, and how membership is reviewed.

modules.title: The RFC pipeline and the decision archive
modules.outcome: Submit an RFC, navigate the state machine, understand consensus, and treat the archive as a teaching corpus.

modules.title: Mentoring and sponsorship at the senior level
modules.outcome: Distinguish mentoring from sponsorship, time-box relationships, recognise mentoring as performance work, and avoid the named failure modes.

modules.title: External voice: writing, speaking, OSS, brand
modules.outcome: Manage the personal-versus-company voice tension across writing, speaking, and OSS contributions.

modules.title: The multi-year horizon
modules.outcome: Understand what the Council shapes that no quarterly artefact captures: the multi-year direction of the AI program.
